duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: CauldronV4 cook() solvency check bypass via unhandled action codes (e.g., ACTION_ACCRUE=8) falling to _additionalCookAction which returns zeroed CookStatus, overwriting needsSolvencyCheck=true
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 9 threats describe the identical code defect: unhandled actions in cook() overwrite the CookStatus struct, clearing needsSolvencyCheck. ECON-H001 selected for most comprehensive attack economics and impact analysis. Note: TECH2-H003 appears twice in input (second instance is this cook bypass).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MagicLpAggregator._getReserves() always returns (0,0) due to local variables shadowing unnamed return values with no explicit return statement, causing latestAnswer() to return 0
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 8 threats describe the same Solidity shadowing bug in _getReserves(). TECH-H002 selected for most precise code analysis with exact line references. Note: TECH2-H004 appears twice in input (second instance is this oracle bug).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: FeeCollectable.setFeeParameters() validates old state variable feeBips instead of new input parameter _feeBips, allowing >100% fee setting and permanent DoS
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 6 threats identify the same variable name mismatch bug in the fee validation check. TECH-H003 selected for clearest technical description of the defect and its cascading effects. Note: TECH2-H004 first instance is this FeeCollectable bug.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Router._validateDecimals() uint8 subtraction underflows when baseDecimals > quoteDecimals, blocking legitimate pool creation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the same uint8 underflow in _validateDecimals. TECH-H004 selected as the most concise and accurate description. Note: TECH2-H007 appears twice in input (second instance is this decimals bug).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: DegenBox.transferMultiple() only validates tos[0] != address(0), allowing shares sent to address(0) at later indices to be permanently locked
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the same partial validation bug in transferMultiple. TECH-H005 selected for best evidence. Note: TECH2-H008 appears twice in input (second instance is this transferMultiple bug).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: CauldronV4.liquidate() double-computes allBorrowShare via BentoBox.toShare() with swapper callback between computations, enabling manipulation of MIM totals to reduce liquidator payment
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 4 threats describe the same TOCTOU vulnerability in the liquidation share computation around the swapper callback. TECH-H006 selected for most detailed technical analysis of the double-computation pattern.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BlastOnboardingBoot._claimable() sums raw MIM and USDB locked amounts 1:1 despite I=0.998 pool pricing, creating disproportionate share allocation favoring MIM depositors
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the same incorrect 1:1 token valuation in _claimable(). ECON-H006 selected for best economic impact analysis. Note: TECH2-H007 first instance is this share allocation bug.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LockingMultiRewards processExpiredLocks swap-and-pop corrupts lastLockIndex and array ordering, causing _createLock to create excess entries and premature maxLocks exhaustion
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 4 threats describe the same swap-and-pop array corruption issue in processExpiredLocks. OPS2-H009 focuses on batch processing but stems from the same root mechanism. TECH-H008 selected for most complete description of the lastLockIndex corruption path.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: CauldronV4 blacklistedCallees set at init time becomes stale when BentoBox ownership is transferred, leaving new owner un-blacklisted for ACTION_CALL
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the same static blacklist that doesn't track BentoBox ownership changes. TECH-H009 selected for deployment script evidence confirming ownership transfer occurs.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MagicLP sellBase/sellQuote/flashLoan use tx.origin instead of msg.sender for fee rate model lookup, enabling fee spoofing and breaking smart contract wallet compatibility
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 5 threats describe the same tx.origin usage in fee rate calculation. TECH-H010 selected for most comprehensive analysis. Note: TECH2-H006 first instance (tx.origin) is removed; second instance (Router _validatePath) is a different vulnerability and retained.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: DegenBox public harvest() enables flash-loan sandwich attack to capture strategy yield by depositing before harvest and withdrawing after
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same flash-loan yield sniping via deposit→harvest→withdraw. ECON-H015 selected for more detailed attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BlastBox rebasing tokens not registered in BlastTokenRegistry default to AUTOMATIC yield mode, allowing anyone to skim accrued yield via DegenBox deposit skimming
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same misconfiguration risk where unregistered rebasing tokens leak yield via skim deposits. ECON-H018 selected for more detailed analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MagicLP buyShares permanently DoS'd when totalSupply > 0 but one reserve is depleted to zero, as code falls through both conditional branches leaving shares=0
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same buyShares DoS when one reserve hits zero. ECON2-H012 selected for more complete analysis including economic attack cost and dead shares interaction.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BlastOnboarding.deposit() updates state and performs transferFrom before checking caps, enabling cap-boundary griefing and gas waste
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same check-after-effects pattern in deposit(). TECH2-H005 (the BlastOnboarding cap instance) selected for more detailed analysis. Note: TECH2-H005 has a duplicate ID; the other instance (Unclearable Bad Debt) is a separate vulnerability and retained.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MagicLpAggregator LP price calculation uses spot reserves (baseReserve + quoteReserve) enabling flash loan manipulation to inflate LP collateral value
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same flash-loan oracle manipulation of spot reserves in latestAnswer(). ECON2-H002 selected for better attack economics. Note: TECH2-H003 first instance (flash loan manipulation) is removed; second instance (cook solvency bypass) is also removed in DUP-001.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: DegenBox deposit() with USE_ETHEREUM allows anyone to wrap stuck ETH into WETH shares for themselves since skim check is bypassed for ETH path
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same stuck ETH skimming vulnerability in the ETH deposit path. ECON2-H005 selected for more detailed proof of signal and economics.
